news.summary: California Governor Gavin Newsom recently announced a proposal to halt new enrollments of undocumented immigrants in the state-funded healthcare program to address a $12 billion deficit in the upcoming $322 billion state spending plan for the fiscal year. The freeze, starting in 2026, would prevent low-income adults without legal status from applying for Medi-Cal, California's Medicaid program. Existing enrollees will retain coverage, with changes affecting adults with "unsatisfactory immigration status" from 2027 requiring a $100 monthly premium. Newsom cited high Medicaid spending and economic volatility influenced by federal tariff policies and stock market instability. He highlighted potential revenue losses of $16 billion due to President Trump's economic policies.
news.headline: Gavin Newsom proposes freezing healthcare access for undocumented immigrants in California's $12 billion deficit; existing enrollees remain covered.

news.summary: The global arterial blood filter market was valued at $980 million in 2024 and is projected to grow at a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of approximately 6.5% from 2025 to 2034, reaching around $1.8 billion by 2034. Arterial blood filters are specialized medical devices used in healthcare facilities to remove impurities from arterial blood during surgeries like cardiopulmonary bypass and hemodialysis, ensuring blood quality and patient safety. The market growth is driven by factors such as rising cardiovascular disease rates, technological advancements, increasing surgical procedures, and a focus on patient safety. Key drivers include the global increase in cardiovascular diseases, growth in surgical procedures, technological advancements in filter materials, increased healthcare expenditure, emphasis on blood management and patient safety, aging global population, regulatory support, cost-effectiveness, and competitive dynamics. Market restraints include regulatory hurdles, high manufacturing costs, limited awareness and training in some regions, economic and technological pressures, and global health disruptions. These factors contribute to the growth and challenges faced by the arterial blood filter market.
news.headline: Global arterial blood filter market expected to reach $1.8 billion by 2034, with 6.5% CAGR growth. Market value in 2024 was $980 million. Market driven by rising cardiovascular disease rates, technological advancements, and an increased focus on patient safety in surgical environments. Key drivers include increased incidence of cardiovascular diseases, growth in surgical procedures, technological advancements, increased healthcare expenditure, emphasis on blood management and patient safety, aging global population, regulatory support, cost-effectiveness, and competitive dynamics. Market faces challenges including regulatory hurdles, high manufacturing costs, limited awareness and training, economic and technological pressures, and global health disruptions.
INDIA'S NEW DELHI CITY GOVERNMENT REJECTS PERNOD'S LIQUOR LICENCE REQUEST FOR A THIRD TIME - DOCUMENT

news.summary: In Los Angeles, Capstone Green Energy Holdings, Inc. (CGEH) and Capstone Green Energy, LLC. revealed that Vergent Power, Capstone’s distributor for the Upper Midwest, New England, and Eastern Canada, has successfully obtained a new order for the City of Janesville, Wisconsin. The order involves the installation of additional Capstone microturbines to upgrade the city's wastewater treatment facility. The City of Janesville, a loyal client who has been utilizing Capstone microturbines for several years, will be enhancing their operations with this new installation. The exact number of microturbines or the monetary value of the order was not specified in the announcement.
news.headline: City of Janesville invests in sustainability with new Microturbines from Capstone Green Energy; Follow-on order secured for wastewater treatment facility in Wisconsin. #CleanPower

news.summary: Philadelphia-based company PECO, a subsidiary of Exelon (Nasdaq: EXC), has revealed that its President and CEO, David Velazquez, will be retiring. Velazquez, a seasoned Exelon employee with over 40 years of experience in the energy sector, took on the role in February 2024. Hailing from Pennsylvania, Velazquez has been actively involved in the local service area, fostering connections with businesses and community groups. Under his leadership, PECO has reinforced its dedication to the region.
news.headline: PECO's President and CEO, David Velazquez, retires after more than 40 years of service in the energy industry.

news.summary: A recent report reveals that over 3 billion people across the globe are at risk of contracting dengue fever. The disease, caused by a virus transmitted through mosquito bites, affects around 390 million individuals annually, leading to approximately 20,000 deaths. Scientists warn that climate change may exacerbate the spread of the disease, as warmer temperatures allow mosquitoes to thrive in new regions. With no specific treatment available, prevention efforts such as mosquito control and public awareness campaigns are crucial in combating this global health threat.
news.headline: Alibaba's growth disappoints, signaling challenges for Chinese tech industry.
U.S. VICE PRESIDENT VANCE TO ATTEND POPE LEO INAUGURATL MASS -STATEMENT

news.summary: An analyst warned that if Powell does not indicate a need to quickly implement policy changes, markets may react defensively.
news.headline: Bitcoin prices drop due to weaker US wholesale prices; analyst warns of defensive market if Powell doesn't signal policy ease.

news.summary: Ideal Power Inc., a provider of innovative semiconductor power switch technology, reported its first quarter results for 2025. They completed SSCB prototypes three months ahead of schedule and secured an order from a Forbes Global 500 power management market leader for DC microgrid circuit protection. They also announced potential new EV projects with Stellantis and secured orders for B-TRAN devices from various customers. Despite higher R&D spending, operating expenses were $2.8 million with a net loss of $2.7 million in Q1 2025. Cash used in operations was $2.1 million, with cash and equivalents at $13.7 million. Revenue was $12,003 in Q1 2025. The company has set milestones for 2025, including securing further developments with Stellantis and increasing product power ratings. Their B-TRAN technology has 94 issued patents and a global presence. Interested parties can join the conference call on May 15 to discuss these results. The company focuses on eco-friendly energy solutions for various applications. Investors should consider the risks and uncertainties outlined in their reports. For more information about Ideal Power Inc., visit their website.
news.headline: Ideal Power reported Q1 2025 results, with $2.7 million net loss, $12,003 commercial revenue, $2.8 million operating expenses, and $13.7 million cash. Key milestones include securing next phase of development with Stellantis and increasing power rating of products. Conference call scheduled for May 15, 2025. For more info, visit www.IdealPower.com.

news.summary: Mersana Therapeutics provided a business update and reported financial results for the first quarter of 2025. They presented updated clinical data from Emi-Le Phase 1 trials at the ESMO Breast Cancer 2025, showing an increase in the Objective Response Rate (ORR) to 31% among patients with B7-H4 high tumors. They focused on TNBC patients receiving intermediate doses, revealing an ORR of 29%, median PFS of 16.0 weeks, and median OS not reached, correcting the previous report of 1.7 months to 5.7 months. It was noted that the performance of standard care for TNBC patients previously treated with topo-1 ADC was poor, creating a significant unmet medical need. The upcoming ASCO presentation will provide further insights into the clinical data. Mersana is advancing into the dose expansion phase and plans to report initial clinical data later in 2025. They have made progress in patient enrollment in the dose expansion cohorts. The Phase 1 trial of XMT-2056 is also ongoing.
news.headline: Mersana Therapeutics reported first quarter financial results with an increase in ORR to 31% across tumor types. Initial data from expansion expected in second half of 2025 - emphasized at ESMO Breast Cancer 2025. Expansion ongoing for Emi-Le in TNBC - showing promising results with a corrected Median OS of 5.7 months. Expanding patient population for post-topo-1 breast cancer. Updates presented at ASCO 2025 conference.
INDIA TRADE MINISTRY SOURCE: INDIA REMAINS COMPETITIVE AS A SMARTPHONE MANUFACTURING HUB, APPLE LIKELY TO TAKE OWN DECISION

news.summary: In Ontario, a collaboration between Diageo and BITS has provided assistance to more than 1,200 entrepreneurs by offering training and resources over the last three years.
news.headline: Diageo Canada and BITS partner to support 1,200+ entrepreneurs in Ontario with training and resources in 3 years.

news.summary: In Charlotte, North Carolina, Akoustis Technologies, Inc., a leading integrated device manufacturer of patented bulk acoustic wave high-band RF filters for mobile and wireless applications, has finalized the sale of most of its assets to Tune Holdings Corp. The sale includes the transfer of assets from wholly-owned subsidiaries Akoustis, Inc. and RFM Integrated Device Inc. The successful completion of the sale marks a significant milestone for Akoustis, positioning the Company for future growth and development in the industry.
news.headline: Akoustis completes sale of assets to SpaceX, per Business Wire, Charlotte, NC. Important numbers: assets sold to Tune Holdings.

news.summary: The global thermal ablation system market, valued at approximately $4.2 billion in 2024, is projected to reach $7.5 billion by 2034 with a robust Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 6.4%. Leading technologies in this market include radiofrequency ablation (45% market share), microwave ablation systems (30% market share), laser ablation systems (15% market share), and high-intensity focused ultrasound (HIFU) (10% market share). North America leads with a strong healthcare infrastructure and high incidence rates of chronic diseases, while Europe and Asia-Pacific follow with growing adoption. Hospitals hold 60% of the market share, while clinics are growing in popularity due to outpatient procedures. Key drivers include rising chronic diseases, advancements in imaging technology, and a preference for minimally invasive treatments.
news.headline: Global thermal ablation systems market to hit $7.5B by 2034 with 6.4% CAGR growth, led by North America, $4.2B in 2024. RFA holds 45% market share.
US Industrial Production (M/M) Apr: 0.0% (est 0.1%; prev -0.3%) Manufacturing (SIC) Production (M/M): -0.4% (est -0.3%; prev R 0.4%)

news.summary: The semiconductor synthetic quartz ingots market in Luton, Bedfordshire, United Kingdom, is expected to grow significantly, reaching a valuation of $2.5 billion by 2024 and projected to reach $4.1 billion by 2034 with a 5.2% Compound Annual Growth Rate. This growth is driven by increasing demand for high-purity quartz for semiconductor production due to technologies like cloud computing, AI, and IoT, along with the rise in renewable energy sources and electric cars. Key manufacturers are Shin-Etsu, Feilihua, and others. Asia-Pacific dominates the market, followed by Europe and the United States. The market is influenced by innovations in production methods, stringent regulations, and competition from alternative materials like silicon carbide. Major end-users are semiconductor giants, solar energy leaders, and tech innovators. The industry faces challenges like high production costs and environmental compliance burdens, but growth drivers include rising demand in electronics, automotive, and data centers. Key trends include the use of AI to optimize processes.
news.headline: Market for semiconductor synthetic quartz ingots to reach $4.1 billion by 2034 with 5.2% CAGR. Dominated by Asia-Pacific, key manufacturers are Shin-Etsu, Feilihua, Tosoh, Heraeus Conamic. Regulatory impact includes stringent environmental laws. Competitive landscape showcases rising alternative materials. Major end users include Samsung, Apple, Intel. Industry faces high production costs and environmental challenges but benefits from technological advances and rising demand in electronics and solar energy sectors.

news.summary: The global medical bioreactor market is valued at $4.1 billion in 2024 and is estimated to reach $7.2 billion by 2034, growing at a CAGR of 6.2% from 2025 to 2034. Key drivers of this growth include the increasing demand for biopharmaceuticals, breakthroughs in tissue engineering and regenerative medicine. The surge in biopharmaceutical production, advancements in regenerative medicine, and growing investments in biotechnology are contributing to the market expansion. Laboratory bioreactors are crucial for drug development, with North America leading the market and Asia-Pacific expected to register the fastest growth. The market is segmented by type, application, workflow, end-user, product, and mode of operation, presenting opportunities in biopharmaceutical production and regenerative medicine. However, high costs and the complexity of operations may pose threats to market growth. Competitive players in the market include Sartorius AG, Thermo Fisher Scientific, and GE Healthcare.
news.headline: Global medical bioreactor market valued at $4.1 billion in 2024, projected to reach $7.2 billion by 2034, with a CAGR of 6.2%. Major drivers include biopharmaceutical production, regenerative medicine advancements, growing biotech investments. North America leads, Asia-Pacific shows fastest growth. Major players include Sartorius AG, Thermo Fisher Scientific, GE Healthcare.

news.summary: Bitcoin's price is currently down 1.4% from its all-time high of $109,000, but strong ETF inflows totaling $2.9 billion over the last two weeks suggest a bullish trend towards $140,000. The CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) is dropping, indicating a "risk-on" sentiment for Bitcoin. Whale accumulation is increasing, with large investors accumulating significantly more Bitcoin in the past month. Exchange balances are decreasing, suggesting investors are withdrawing coins into self-custody wallets. High network activity and a rising transaction volume Z-Score further support the potential for Bitcoin to rally towards $140,000, with technical indicators also pointing towards a bullish breakout. Overall, these factors indicate positive momentum for Bitcoin's price and investor confidence in the cryptocurrency.
news.headline: Bitcoin retracing but strong ETF inflows, high network activity, and whale accumulation predict $140K price top. BTC down 1.4% in 24 hours, 6% below ATH $109K. Spot Bitcoin ETFs saw $2.9B in net inflows in 2 weeks. Exchange balances declining, rising transaction volume Z-Score. BTC accumulation trend score at 6-year low, suggesting bullish sentiment. Potential for $140K, RSI at 70 supporting upside. Source: Analysts.

news.summary: Comstock Holding Companies, Inc., a prominent asset manager, developer, and operator of mixed-use properties in the Washington, D.C. area, unveiled its 2025 Investor Presentation. The presentation is now available to the public on the company's investor relations website. You can access the full presentation at the following link: https://ir.comstock.com/events-and-presentation. Comstock Holding Companies, Inc. is listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol CHCI.
news.headline: Comstock, Nasdaq: CHCI, releases 2025 Investor Presentation on mixed-use properties in D.C. region. Link for presentation: https://ir.comstock.com/events-and-presentation

news.summary: In Scottsdale, Arizona, Carlisle Companies Incorporated (NYSE:CSL) has confirmed their acquisition of certain assets from Bonded Logic, Inc. and Phoenix Fibers, LLC. This deal includes the takeover of the sustainable thermal and acoustical insulation products division of Bonded Logic, a prominent U.S. manufacturer. Established in 2002, Bonded Logic operates manufacturing facilities in Arizona and Georgia, specializing in natural fiber insulation products.This acquisition marks a significant development in the industry, positioning Carlisle Companies as a key player in the insulation market.
news.headline: Carlisle Companies buys Bonded Logic, a sustainable insulation products maker with assets from Phoenix Fibers, for undisclosed amount.

news.summary: CoreWeave Inc (NASDAQ: CRWV) released its inaugural quarterly results as a publicly traded company. Shares surged initially but later dipped due to plans for significant expenditures. Key details include a 420% year-over-year revenue increase to $981.63 million for the first quarter, surpassing the projected $859.77 million. The Nvidia-backed AI infrastructure firm disclosed an adjusted loss of about 60 cents per share. By the end of the quarter, CoreWeave boasted a $25.9 billion revenue backlog, with a notable $11.2 billion stemming from a deal with OpenAI. The stock hit record highs in after-hours trading, but retreated following the spending strategy outlined during the conference call.
news.headline: CoreWeave (CRWV) reports 1Q revenue up 420% YoY to $981.63M, beats estimates. Adjusted loss approx. 60 cents/share. Revenue backlog $25.9B. Shares hit new highs, pulled back after spending plans.

news.summary: Kartoon Studios reported strong financial results for the first quarter of 2025, with total revenue increasing by 56.4% year-over-year. Mainframe Animation Unit achieved profitability, with revenue up 138% over the prior year quarter and 29% sequentially. The company's operating loss improved by 51.1% year-over-year and 11.9% sequentially. Kartoon Channel! remains the #1 viewer-ranked kids' streaming app in the Apple App Store, outperforming competitors like Netflix and YouTube Kids. The company has zero long-term debt and a streamlined cost structure. Mainframe Studios has deep partnerships with industry leaders and is currently producing content for major franchises like Barbie and CoComelon. Kartoon Channel! has experienced doubled watch time year-over-year and has a global reach of over 1.5 billion potential viewers across 61 countries. The company is poised for investor growth with profitable units and valuable IP projects set to create long-term shareholder value.
news.headline: Kartoon Studios revenue up 56% in Q1 2025, with mainframe animation unit up 138%, poised for profit by year-end.

news.summary: BranchOut Food Inc. announced a record Q1 2025 revenue of $3.2 million, up 118% from the previous year. The company aims to be debt-free by YE 2025. Their Peru facility is now fully operational, supporting over $40 million in annual production capacity. They have expanded their Warehouse Club partnership to five U.S. regions, driving nearly $3 million in sales in H1 2025. A strategic ingredient partnership with MicroDried is expected to bring in $5-6 million in annual ingredient sales. The company is benefiting from tariff advantages, with tariffs at 30% on imports from China. BranchOut's production in Peru and U.S. retail packaging offer cost advantages. The company has appointed a Chief Brand Officer to focus on launching the DTC & Brand Strategy as they anticipate strong growth throughout the year.
news.headline: BranchOut Food Inc. reports record $3.2M in Q1 revenue, up 118%. Peru factory operational, aiming to be debt-free by 2025. Strategic partnerships and expansion driving growth.

news.summary: JX Luxventure Group Inc., a company listed on NASDAQ as JXG, operating in the wholesale trade sector with a focus on duty-free and cross-border consumer goods, released its financial results for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2024. The revenue for the year stood at $49,840,288, showing a substantial increase of $17,999,700 from the previous year and reflecting a 57% year-over-year growth. The gross profit reached $8,352,316, a rise of $2,895,947 from 2023, indicating a 53% increase. Net profit was $3,073,807, a slight increase of $30,028 from the previous year, demonstrating a 1% year-over-year growth. Ms. Sun "Ice" Lei, the Chief Executive Officer of the Company, expressed satisfaction with the results.
news.headline: JX Luxventure Group reports 57% revenue growth in FY 2024, with revenue at $49.8 million and gross profit at $8.35 million.

news.summary: In Dallas, RGP has appointed Jennifer Jones as its inaugural Chief Marketing Officer (CMO). This development marks a significant milestone for the organization.
news.headline: RGP appoints Jennifer Jones as first Chief Marketing Officer.
US APRIL INDUSTRIAL PRODUCTION UNCHANGED M/M; EST. +0.1%

news.summary: In a recent development, Savi has unveiled an innovative AI Assistant tool that is now accessible to millions of union members through a collaborative effort with Union Plus.
news.headline: Savi launches first AI assistant for student loans, offering relief to millions of borrowers in partnership with Union Plus.

news.summary: Iodine Software, a prominent player in healthcare AI for revenue cycle optimization, has unveiled the latest version of its pre-bill tool in Austin, Texas. Known as AwarePre-Bill, this solution is crafted to aid health systems in bridging significant revenue shortfalls before claims are finalized. Drawing on a track record of effectiveness, this offering empowers health systems to perform retrospective reviews that target financial aspects, thus enhancing code precision and documentation accuracy to optimize reimbursement levels. Through Iodine, customers gain access to a platform that facilitates this crucial financial review process.
news.headline: Iodine Software launches AwarePre-Bill solution to close revenue gaps, improve code accuracy for right-sizing reimbursement.

news.summary: EnerSys, a renowned global provider of energy solutions for industrial needs, was recognized for its exceptional achievement at the Data Centre Review Excellence Awards 2025. The company, listed on NYSE as ENS, claimed victory in the Data Centre Backup Power Solution of the Year category. This recognition was received at a prestigious event held on May 14 at London's Oxo Tower. EnerSys delegates accepted the award with pride amidst a panel of esteemed judges.
news.headline: EnerSys (NYSE: ENS) honored with Data Centre Backup Power Solution of the Year award at DCR Excellence Awards 2025.

news.summary: Houston-based energy company Phillips 66 has recently revealed a deal to sell off a significant portion of its retail marketing business in Germany and Austria. This agreement involves a transfer of majority ownership stake. The move is part of the company’s strategic plan for restructuring its operations and focusing on key markets. The specific details of the deal, including the percentage of ownership being divested and the financial terms, have not been disclosed yet.
news.headline: Phillips 66 to sell majority stake in Germany, Austria retail business.

news.summary: The recent price surge of Ethereum (ETH) has sparked optimism in the altcoin market, as analysts predict a full-scale altcoin bull market by 2025. Matthew Sigel, Head of Digital Assets Research at VanEck, noted during a Wolf of All Streets podcast episode with Scott Melker that Ethereum's 44% weekly increase has shifted the sentiment in the sector. Sigel mentioned that this surge signifies the beginning of an altcoin bull market, highlighting the outperformance of assets like Dogecoin (DOGE) and Solana (SOL) compared to Bitcoin (BTC). He credited the rise of ETH to various catalysts. Information can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Ethereum's surge sparks optimism for altcoins with experts predicting a bull market in 2025. ETH up 44% weekly.
PHILLIPS 66 TO SELL STAKE IN GERMANY, AUSTRIA MARKETING UNIT

news.summary: In Bellevue, Washington, T-Mobile (NASDAQ: TMUS) has announced an enticing iPhone promotion, aiming to simplify the switching process for customers. As part of their latest offer, T-Mobile is providing customers with a complimentary iPhone 16 Pro, with no trade-in required. Additionally, T-Mobile is committed to assisting customers in paying off their existing devices from other carriers by covering up to $800 per line. This limited-time offer marks T-Mobile's most exceptional iPhone deal to date, exclusively available on their Experience Beyond plan, renowned for its value-packed features.
news.headline: Switch to T-Mobile and get a free iPhone 16 Pro, no trade-in required, while they also cover up to $800 per line for current phone payoffs.

news.summary: A partnership has been established between Ingenium and Veolia North America, with the aim of granting access to Veolia’s upcoming high-temperature treatment plant in Gum Springs, Arkansas, set to open in 2025. The purpose of this collaboration is to meet the increasing need within the industry for waste treatment capabilities. As part of the agreement, Ingenium will also have secured access to capacity in Veolia's current thermal, landfill, and fuel blending sites across the United States. This development addresses the necessity for waste management solutions, and is a significant step in ensuring future operational requirements are met effectively.
news.headline: Ingenium and Veolia NA partner for waste treatment capacity in Arkansas, new facility by 2025, guaranteed access to Veolia's existing facilities in US.

news.summary: New York-based White Oak Commercial Finance (WOCF), a division of White Oak Global Advisors, has revealed the provision of a $125 million asset-based revolving credit line to a US energy services firm. Acting as the exclusive lender, WOCF has committed the entire $125 million in this deal. This credit facility is designed to bolster the financial maneuverability and cash reserves of the energy company. The company stressed the importance of the energy industry in the nation's economic development and stability.
news.headline: White Oak Commercial Finance provides $125M ABL facility to US energy services company to boost financial flexibility.

news.summary: Retail powerhouse Walmart Inc. (NYSE: WMT) released its first-quarter FY26 financial results on Thursday. The company saw a 2.5% year-on-year sales increase to $165.60 billion, slightly below the anticipated $165.88 billion. Adjusted earnings per share came in at 61 cents, surpassing the expected 58 cents. Global eCommerce sales surged by 22% Y/Y, driven by store-based pickup & delivery services and marketplace growth. Walmart U.S. reported a 4.5% rise in comparable sales, excluding fuel, fueled by health & wellness and grocery sectors. Sam's Club also experienced a 6.7% Y/Y increase in same-store sales, excluding fuel. The gross margin rate increased by 12 basis points, with Walmart U.S. leading the way. Operating income rose by $0.3 billion (4.3% increase), and adjusted operating income climbed by 3.0% in constant currencies. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Walmart's Q1 sales growth was 2.5% to $165.60 billion, eCommerce sales skyrocketed 22% Y/Y, Walmart U.S. comp sales rose by 4.5%, Sam's Club comp sales increased by 6.7%. CEO highlights cost pressures from tariffs, particularly impacting China.

news.summary: Premier, Inc. revealed the names of the nation's top 15 health systems in their recent study. This annual analysis, exclusive to Premier, is the most thorough examination of data for top-performing health systems in the country. The study indicated that if all health systems operated at the same high level as the current top performers, an estimated 171,000 more lives could have been saved, along with over 336,000 additional benefits.
news.headline: Premier Inc. reveals top 15 Health Systems, show they could save over 171,000 lives if all systems operated at same level.

news.summary: In Lehi, Utah, Entrata, a top operating system for multifamily communities, revealed a $200 million minority investment from funds managed by Blackstone, valuing the company at $4.3 billion. This investment marks a significant move for Entrata's growth journey, focusing on enhancing the rental housing industry with advanced technology to simplify property management operations and enhance the living standards for millions of residents. With over twelve million residents benefited, Entrata is geared towards further expansion and innovation.
news.headline: Entrata gets $200M from Blackstone at $4.3B valuation for growth in rental housing technology. Moving ahead with improving operations.

news.summary: In Denver, AIR Communities has achieved its first certification as a Great Place To Work, with a notable 88% of employees expressing satisfaction with their workplace. The recognition, solely determined by feedback from current staff, highlights the organization's commitment to fostering a positive company culture. AIR Communities' Senior Vice President of Human Resources, Beth Ha, credited their focus on building a cohesive and supportive environment for the substantial increase in employee engagement.
news.headline: AIR Communities receives 2025 Great Place To Work Certification with 88% employee affirmation.

news.summary: Champion Homes, Inc. (NYSE: SKY) based in Troy, Michigan, has rescheduled the announcement of its fourth quarter and full-year 2025 earnings results. The new date for the release is on Tuesday, May 27, 2025, before the market opens. A conference call to discuss the financial results will be held at 8:30 A.M. Eastern Time on the same day. Stakeholders and investors can tune in to a live webcast of the call.
news.headline: Champion Homes reschedules Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings report to May 27, 2025 with call at 8:30 A.M. Eastern Time.

news.summary: Stratus Properties Inc., a real estate company operating in Austin, Texas and other Texas markets, has released its first-quarter 2025 financial results. The company reported a net loss of $(2.9) million, or $(0.36) per diluted share, for the quarter. This marks a significant decrease from the previous year's net income of $4.6 million, or $0.56 per diluted share. Stratus Properties Inc. is focused on residential and retail properties in the Austin area and select markets in Texas.
news.headline: Stratus Properties Inc. reports $(2.9) million net loss in first-quarter 2025, compared to $4.6 million net income.
